mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending corporate margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and rising retail sales confirms broad-based consumer spending strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive returns in over 60 percent of triggered quarters, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over single-series baselines (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the industrial production index during the early stages of an economic expansion reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of early-cycle INDPRO surges, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance (Rank IC < 0) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPI inflation reading exceeding 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of months where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5, or the threshold effect shows no statistical significance (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer purchasing power, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS growth signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of quarters, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts combined with a declining unemployment rate signals an overheating domestic economy and rising input costs, which erodes corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST-UNRATE signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered months, or the interaction effect fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC) relative to additive specifications.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in industrial production during the late stages of an economic cycle confirms weakening manufacturing demand and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of late-cycle INDPRO declines, or the signal's out-of-sample predictive accuracy falls below 45 percent in walk-forward testing.
